key_insights.insight: The food matrix (structure of food) significantly impacts how our bodies process and utilize nutrients, even when nutritional labels are identical.
key_insights.quote: You can have two foods with identical backpack labeling that have different effects.

key_insights.insight: Ultra-processed foods are often demonized, but processing can also make food edible, safe, stable, tasty, and convenient.
key_insights.quote: Processing can also be used to our benefit.

key_insights.insight: Slowing down your eating rate by 20% can lead to a reduction in calorie intake of approximately 15% due to hormonal responses.
key_insights.quote: On average, if you change the speed in which you eat your food by about 20%, you reduce your calorie intake by about 15%.

key_insights.insight: Heavily processed, soft-textured foods are often eaten much faster than unprocessed, harder-textured foods, leading to overeating because fullness signals don't catch up quickly enough.
key_insights.quote: Heavily processed, soft textured type food can be eaten 50% more quickly than the unprocessed, harder texturedequivalent.

key_insights.insight: Frequency of eating isn't necessarily bad as long as you are eating healthy foods/snacks, within reason.
key_insights.quote: If people were grazing, having multiple eating events,as long as they were eating healthy foods,it didn't matter having multiple eating events.

key_insights.insight: Snacking late at night (after 9 PM) is associated with unfavorable health outcomes, even when snacking on healthy foods, as it disrupts the body's natural clock.
key_insights.quote: We found that if you snack late at night that that was associated with unfavourable health outcomes.

key_insights.insight: Sleep impacts metabolic responses after eating as much as the macronutrient content of the meal.
key_insights.quote: Sleep affected metabolic responses as much as the macronutrient content of the meal.

key_insights.insight: While any added fiber is better than none, fiber from whole foods in its original structure will always be better.
key_insights.quote: Having fiber though from the whole foodis always going to be better.

key_insights.insight: The seed oil debate is largely misinformation; credible evidence suggests seed oils are not harmful and may even be beneficial.
key_insights.quote: There is absolutely no evidence, that is credible evidence, when interpreted in the correct way, to show seed oils are harmful.

key_insights.insight: During perimenopause, women experience a higher number of symptoms if they have an unhealthy diet to start with and/or live with obesity.
key_insights.quote: We've looked at how living with obesity can impact your symptoms and we do seethat if you're living with obesity you have a higher number of symptoms.

actionable_advice.advice: Slow down your eating speed and chew your food more thoroughly.
actionable_advice.how_to: Consciously take smaller bites, put your fork down between bites, and chew each mouthful at least 20-40 times to trigger fullness signals.

actionable_advice.advice: Avoid snacking late at night, especially after 9 PM.
actionable_advice.how_to: Establish a cut-off time for eating and stick to it, or if hungry, have a glass of water or herbal tea instead. Shift more of your daily calories earlier.

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize whole, unprocessed foods with a high fiber content.
actionable_advice.how_to: Swap refined grains for whole grains, choose whole fruits over juices, and incorporate legumes and nuts into your diet, aiming for at least 30g of fiber daily.
Go to bed and wake up around the same time each day, even on weekends to avoid 'social jetlag' and its negative impacts.

memorable_stories: The 1977 study that compared the effects of whole apples vs apple puree on satiety and blood sugar, highlighting the importance of food matrix.
The study that saw a 30% reduction in cardiovascular disease for participants that substituted usual snacks for almonds.
The slumber study demonstrating how extending sleep can lead to healthier food choices, reducing free sugar intake.
contrarian_claims: The food industry isn't necessarily 'out to get us', and some people are 'very naive' to believe that's the case.
Seed oils are not inherently toxic and can be part of a healthy diet.
The prevailing narrative that scientists can't be trusted for their research if they've taken money from the food industry is incorrect.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Lead Nutritional Scientist: Seed Oils Are Not Bad For You! Eating This Twice A Day Will Help Menopause! The Alarming Link Between Chewing & Belly Fat!
